tagline: Prepare model railway photos for digital control software (Rocrail, iTrain). Upload multiple images, set length per model, process them all in one go.
sidebar.global_settings_caption: These apply to **all** images in the batch.
sidebar.px_per_mm_help: Scale factor. Set ONCE per collection — same value for all vehicles. E.g. 2.0 means a 165 mm coach becomes 330 px wide.
sidebar.canvas_height_help: Output PNG height. Rocrail standard is 80 px.
sidebar.auto_rotate_help: Auto-level the bottom edge. Safe — only applies if 0.2°-5°.
sidebar.auto_perspective_help: Straighten end faces. Experimental — requires opencv-python.
sidebar.add_rail_help: Place a rail under the model.
sidebar.rail_extend_help: If on (default): canvas grows downwards by rail height, rail sits below the wheels. If off: rail is overlaid on the bottom of the wheels (canvas height stays the same).
sidebar.precrop_help: Crop the image BEFORE rembg, to remove distractions.
sidebar.precrop_roi_help: Pixel coordinates in the original photo.
main.upload_help: Drop one or more photos. Use the × button to remove individual files.
main.batch_caption: Edit output name and length per image. To remove an image, click the **×** next to its name in the upload zone above.
main.empty_hint: 👆 Upload images above to get started. You can drag-and-drop multiple files at once.
results.heading: ✨ Results
results.overflow: ⚠️ Image is taller than canvas — clipped at top.
footer.about_text:
**railshot** prepares model railway photos for digital control software like Rocrail or iTrain. Built for **N-scale** (1:160) but works for any scale where the px-per-mm factor is consistent.

**Multi-image workflow:**
1. Drop multiple photos at once into the upload zone
2. For each photo, edit the output name and the model length (mm)
3. Set the global settings (scale, auto-rotate, rail, pre-crop) once
4. Click "Process all"
5. Download individual PNGs or all together as ZIP

**Key concept:** the `Pixels per mm` value defines the scale for your *entire collection*. Set it once, then keep it constant — otherwise different vehicles won't be in the same scale anymore.

Source: [github.com/magliaral/railshot](https://github.com/magliaral/railshot) | License: MIT
